name: Anginares Tarama
recipeInstructions:
1. To clean the artichokes, cut off the stems flush with the base. Remove any tough, unsightly outer leaves and cut off the top one-third of the flower bud itself. Force the leaves open a little and rinse under cold water, using lemon juice or vinegar to prevent discoloring.
2. Place the artichokes upside-down in a pot large enough to hold them all in one layer. Pour in boiling water until it comes three-quarters of the way up the sides. Simmer for 15-40 minutes, depending on their age and size, until tender when pricked with a fork. Drain and refresh under cold water. Repeat draining and chill.
3. Beat the tarama or smoked cod's roe with grated onion in a food processor. If using by hand, it may take perseverance to make it light.
4. Grate zest of one lemon and add to the mixture, along with 2 teaspoons boiling water.
5. Squeeze juice from both lemons. Alternately dribble in lemon juice and olive oil, beating each addition before adding the next, as if making mayonnaise. Add pepper to taste.
6. Open the artichokes and remove the inner, soft leaves and hairy choke. Spoon tarama into the leaves.
